title: The University of Hong Kong (HKU) Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026: Fully Funded Visiting Fellowship for Scholars from the Global South (Hong Kong)
summary: The HKU Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026 is a prestigious academic opportunity designed to foster international collaboration, research excellence, and knowledge exchange among scholars from the Global South. Hosted by the Faculty of Social Sciences at the University of Hong Kong (HKU), this fellowship enables researchers to engage in meaningful academic partnerships, contribute to global scholarship, and strengthen cross-cultural academic networks. This initiative reflects HKU’s commitment to internationalization, interdisciplinarity, and global impact , creating a dynamic environment where scholars can collaborate on innovative research and share insights that address pressing global challenges. About the HKU Global South Fellowship Scheme The HKU Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme is designed to: Promote academic exchange between HKU and scholars from the Global South Foster collaborative research projects with long-term impact Strengthen global academic networks and partnerships Encourage interdisciplinary knowledge sharing Fellows will be hosted at HKU’s Faculty of Social Sciences and will actively participate in research, teaching, and academic engagement activities. Fellowship Duration and Timeline The fellowship offers flexible visiting periods aligned with HKU’s academic calendar: Duration: 1 to 3 months Available Periods: Fall Semester: September to January Spring Semester: February to June Application Timeline Applications are accepted twice per year , based on the intended visiting period: Fall Semester: Call for Applications: April Deadline: May Announcement: By end of June Spring Semester: Call for Applications: September Deadline: October Announcement: By end of November Current Application Deadline: May 31, 2026 (Hong Kong Time) Fellowship Benefits and Financial Support Selected fellows receive a comprehensive support package, including: Travel Support Economy class return airfare up to HK$10,000 (reimbursable) Daily Allowance HK$1,500 per day (covers accommodation and living expenses) Academic and Institutional Support Access to HKU facilities Opportunities to collaborate with leading academics Participation in seminars, lectures, and research activities Expected Activities and Responsibilities Fellows are expected to actively contribute to HKU’s academic environment through: Collaborative Research Work with a Faculty Associate or other HKU faculty members Produce at least one co-authored publication within one year Academic Engagement Deliver lectures or seminars Participate in networking events and academic discussions Provide training or mentorship to students Knowledge Sharing Contribute to intra-fellow exchange sessions Engage in interdisciplinary collaboration Reporting Requirements Submit a completion report within 3 months after the fellowship Share publication outcomes with the Faculty Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be professoriate staff (e.g., lecturers, assistant professors, or higher) Be affiliated with a university or comparable institution Be based in a Global South country Demonstrate strong academic and research achievements Preference is given to early-career or junior scholars with potential for long-term collaboration. Why You Should Apply The HKU Global South Fellowship offers: A prestigious international academic experience Opportunities for high-impact research collaboration Access to a global network of scholars Financial support for travel and living expenses Enhanced academic visibility and career advancement Application Process To apply for the fellowship, applicants must follow these steps: Identify a Faculty Associate Contact an HKU Faculty member with similar research interests The Faculty Associate must be an Assistant Professor or above Initiate Contact Send an email expressing interest in collaboration Suggested subject line: “HKU FoSS Global South Fellowship Scheme” Prepare Application Develop a research proposal and supporting documents Submit Application Complete the official Online Application Form before the deadline Selection Process Up to 10 fellows are selected per academic year Selection is based on: Academic track record Research achievements Potential for collaboration and impact Contact Information For inquiries and additional support: Email: fossres@hku.hk Click HERE for more information about the HKU Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026. Conclusion The HKU Social Sciences Global South Fellowship Scheme 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for scholars from the Global South to engage in meaningful academic exchange, collaborative research, and global knowledge production . By participating in this fellowship, researchers can expand their academic horizons, contribute to impactful scholarship, and build long-lasting international partnerships that shape the future of social sciences. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE . title: Football Innovation Analyst Job at Right to Dream: A Global Opportunity to Shape the Future of Football Performance
summary: The world of football is rapidly evolving, driven by innovation, data, and cutting-edge performance strategies. If you are passionate about transforming how football is played and analyzed, the Football Innovation Analyst role at Right to Dream (RTD) offers a unique, career-defining opportunity. This position places you at the heart of elite football development, where creativity meets analytics to produce real, measurable impact on the pitch. With a presence spanning multiple continents, Right to Dream continues to redefine talent development and performance excellence. About Right to Dream Right to Dream is a globally recognized football organization dedicated to developing young talent through academies and professional clubs. Operating across countries such as Denmark, Egypt, and Ghana, RTD combines education, sport, and innovation to create future football leaders. Their approach goes beyond traditional training by integrating data-driven insights, modern coaching techniques, and a strong commitment to personal development. Job Overview: Football Innovation Analyst The Football Innovation Analyst role is designed for forward-thinking professionals who thrive at the intersection of: Football performance Data analytics Tactical innovation Research and development This is not a traditional analyst role. Instead, it is a strategic and hands-on position focused on embedding innovation into real football environments. Location and Mobility Location: Flexible Travel: International travel required Key Countries Involved: Denmark Egypt Ghana The role requires spending 3–4 periods per year on-site within academies and clubs to ensure effective implementation of strategies and to build strong working relationships. Key Responsibilities As a Football Innovation Analyst, your work will directly influence team performance and player development. Your responsibilities include: 1. Leading Innovation in Set-Piece Performance Drive research and innovation in set-piece strategies across RTD academies and clubs Identify performance gaps and introduce improvements 2. Collaborating with Technical Teams Work closely with coaches, analysts, and technical staff Define key performance questions and priorities 3. Performance Analysis and Strategy Development Analyze existing routines and tactical setups Design and test new strategies to enhance match outcomes 4. Translating Insights into Action Convert complex data and research into practical, easy-to-implement solutions Ensure insights lead to tangible improvements on the pitch 5. Cross-Club Knowledge Sharing Act as a connector across RTD environments Share best practices and innovations between academies and clubs 6. Advanced Data and AI Utilization Use performance data, video analysis, and AI-supported tools Stay ahead of emerging trends in football analytics 7. Match and Opponent Analysis Conduct detailed match reviews and opponent scouting when required 8. Long-Term Research and Development Explore global football trends Focus on marginal gains that create competitive advantages Minimum Requirements To be successful in this role, candidates must meet the following criteria: Professional Experience At least 3 years of experience in: Football analysis Data analytics Performance research Technical Expertise Strong experience using: Performance data Video analysis tools AI-supported technologies Football Environment Exposure Experience working in: Elite academies Professional football clubs High-performance environments Innovation and Strategy Proven ability to: Design and implement performance initiatives Drive innovation in sports environments Core Skills Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities Excellent communication skills Cultural awareness and adaptability Curiosity and a continuous improvement mindset Reporting Structure Reports to: Group Head of Football – RTD Group This ensures alignment with the organization’s strategic vision and performance objectives. Why This Opportunity Stands Out This role is more than just a job—it is a chance to: Work at the forefront of football innovation and analytics Collaborate with elite coaches and technical leaders Influence real match outcomes and player development Operate in a global, multicultural environment Contribute to shaping the future of football performance Application Deadline Deadline: 18th May 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early to maximize their chances. How to Apply Candidates who are passionate about football innovation and meet the requirements are invited to apply through the official Right to Dream careers platform. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Football Innovation Analyst Job at Right to Dream: A Global Opportunity to Shape the Future of Football Performance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Finverse Fellowship 2026: Apply for Up to $10,000 Funding for Financial Health Projects in Asia
summary: Organizations working to improve financial health outcomes across Asia now have an exciting opportunity to access funding, learning resources, and global recognition through the Finverse Fellowship 2026 . The fellowship, supported by data.org , is designed for financial-health-focused organizations that want to apply Finverse recommendations in real-world projects. Selected organizations can receive up to USD 10,000 in microgrant funding , along with mentorship, networking opportunities, and visibility to help scale their impact. If your organization is tackling financial inclusion, lending access, women’s savings programs, fintech innovation, or financial wellness initiatives, this could be the perfect opportunity. What is the Finverse Fellowship? The Finverse Fellowship supports organizations committed to improving financial health by using AI, data, and Finverse recommendations to strengthen their projects. The program provides: A microgrant of up to USD 10,000 Learning resources and certificate courses Access to the Data and AI Fellows community Mentorship and networking opportunities Visibility across data.org channels Recognition for impactful work in the financial health sector Each selected organization will appoint an internal Finverse Champion , who becomes the featured Finverse Fellow and serves as the main contact for documenting project progress and lessons learned. Benefits for Organizations Selected organizations will enjoy several benefits, including: 1. Funding Support Organizations can receive up to USD 10,000 to support financial health projects that apply Finverse recommendations to improve outcomes and scale impact. 2. AI Learning and Implementation Participants gain opportunities to learn how to better use AI and data tools in their daily operations and long-term strategies. 3. Visibility and Recognition Organizations will receive public recognition for their impact through data.org’s promotional channels and sector visibility platforms. 4. Employee Development The fellowship contributes positively to staff professional growth, engagement, and leadership development. Benefits for Fellows The selected internal fellow (Finverse Champion) will also receive direct benefits such as: Access to the Data and AI Fellows Community Learn alongside other professionals working at the intersection of data, AI, and social impact. Curated Learning Resources Gain access to: Certificate courses Mentorship opportunities data.org educational resources Exclusive Events and Workshops Attend special workshops, networking sessions, and showcase events hosted by data.org. Public Recognition Featured fellows may be highlighted on: data.org website LinkedIn Newsletters Public dissemination platforms Project Funding Support Fellows lead the implementation of the funded financial health project within their organizations. Fellow Responsibilities Selected fellows are expected to commit to: Monthly project check-ins Creating a project plan using Finverse recommendations Documenting their learning journey and project progress Acting as a Finverse ambassador in their professional community Supporting outreach efforts to spotlight their work and organization Who Should Apply? The fellowship is ideal for professionals and organizations working on projects that improve financial health outcomes. Eligible Applicants Include: Financial Service Providers (FSPs) Fintech companies Banks Credit unions Financial inclusion nonprofits NGOs focused on financial health Eligible Countries Projects must be based in one of the following countries: India Indonesia Malaysia Philippines Singapore Example Projects Supported Some examples of suitable projects include: Women’s Savings Programs An NGO in Indonesia collecting attendance and transaction data for women’s savings groups but struggling to understand participant drop-off. Lending Risk Prediction A lending platform in the Philippines with disconnected borrower data systems seeking to identify default risks earlier. These examples show how organizations can use data and AI to improve decision-making and financial outcomes. Key Dates Applications Reviewed on a Rolling Basis Important deadlines include: Apply by: May 29, 2026 Selection by: June 10, 2026 Virtual Fellow Meet: July 2026 Project Progress Check-in: August 12, 2026 Early application is strongly encouraged since reviews happen on a rolling basis. Why You Should Apply If your organization is working to advance financial health and wants funding plus expert support to apply AI-driven solutions, the Finverse Fellowship 2026 offers a strong platform for growth. Beyond funding, it provides learning, mentorship, and recognition that can help your project scale sustainably and create long-term impact. This is more than a grant—it’s an opportunity to become part of a larger movement shaping the future of financial inclusion. How to Apply Interested organizations should submit their Expression of Interest before the deadline. Apply Now Submit your interest before May 29, 2026 to be considered for this opportunity. Organizations that apply early may benefit from faster review and stronger consideration during rolling selections. Discover similar Fellowship opportunities Here. title: IDRC Step Change Final Evaluation 2026: Apply for the 9-Month Program Evaluation Contract Before May 20
summary: The International Development Research Centre (IDRC) has officially opened applications for the Step Change Final Evaluation under Solicitation Number 26270002 , inviting qualified teams to submit proposals for a comprehensive evaluation of the Step Change Program. This procurement opportunity is ideal for consulting firms, research institutions, evaluation experts, and organizations with strong experience in program assessment, development evaluation, and impact measurement. The selected team will be responsible for conducting the final evaluation of the Step Change program based on the requirements outlined in the official Statement of Work. Opportunity Summary Opportunity Title: Step Change Final Evaluation Solicitation Number: 26270002 Organization: International Development Research Centre (IDRC) Publication Date: April 23, 2026 Last Amendment Date: April 27, 2026 Closing Date: May 20, 2026 Closing Time: 13:00 EDT Status: Open Proposed Start Date: July 1, 2026 Contract Duration: 9 Months About the Assignment IDRC is seeking proposals from a qualified evaluation team to undertake the final evaluation of the Step Change Program , a strategic initiative requiring a detailed review of implementation, outcomes, effectiveness, and lessons learned. The successful contractor will deliver evaluation services as described in the official Statement of Work and provide recommendations that will support future programming and decision-making. This opportunity presents a valuable chance for organizations specializing in: Monitoring and Evaluation (M E) International Development Gender and Social Inclusion Programs Research and Policy Analysis Program Impact Assessment Final Project Evaluations Trade Agreement Information This procurement falls under the: Canadian Free Trade Agreement (CFTA) The notice states that there is no reason for limited tendering , meaning the opportunity is open for eligible bidders under standard procurement procedures. Partnering Opportunity for Businesses Businesses interested in collaborating on this tender may partner with other organizations to strengthen their proposals. CanadaBuys provides a partnership listing feature where companies can indicate their interest in teaming up for this opportunity. However, this does not replace the formal bidding process. All interested organizations must still submit their proposals and meet the required evaluation criteria. Available Tender Documents Applicants can access the following official documents: 1. RFP26270002 Step Change Final Evaluation (English) Primary English Request for Proposal document outlining full submission requirements. 2. DDP26270002 Évaluation du programme Activer le changement (French) French version of the tender documentation. 3. Amendment Document (English and French) Contains updates and modifications issued on April 27, 2026. Reviewing all amendment documents is essential before submission. Eligibility and Submission Information Government of Canada tender notices and solicitation documents are: Fully accessible Available free of charge No registration required on CanadaBuys Applicants are strongly encouraged to access documents directly from the official CanadaBuys platform to ensure they are using the most accurate and up-to-date information. Third-party sources may distribute tender notices earlier, but the Government of Canada is not responsible for documents accessed outside the official CanadaBuys portal. Why This Opportunity Matters Winning this contract offers: International consulting exposure Collaboration with IDRC and Government of Canada procurement systems High-value professional evaluation work Strong portfolio enhancement for consulting and research firms Opportunity to influence future development programming For evaluation professionals and firms, this is a strong opportunity to secure a high-impact international assignment. CLICK HERE ON THE BIDDING DETAILS Final Deadline Submit your proposal before: May 20, 2026 at 13:00 EDT Late submissions may not be considered, so early preparation is strongly recommended. Pro Tip for Applicants Carefully review the amendment document before preparing your proposal. Many applicants miss important updates released after the initial posting, which can affect eligibility, technical requirements, or submission instructions. Also, if your firm lacks some required expertise, consider forming a strategic partnership with another qualified organization to strengthen your bid. The IDRC Step Change Final Evaluation 2026 is a strong consulting and procurement opportunity for organizations with expertise in evaluation, development programming, and impact assessment. With a 9-month contract period and an internationally recognized client, this tender provides both professional visibility and meaningful work. Interested teams should begin preparing early and ensure full compliance with the RFP requirements before the May 20 deadline. Discover similar opportunities Here. title: W.K. Kellogg Foundation Grants 2026: Open Funding Opportunities for Organizations Advancing Equity, Child Wellbeing, and Community Development
summary: The W.K. Kellogg Foundation (WKKF) offers one of the most impactful and flexible global funding opportunities for organizations working to create equitable, thriving communities for children and families . Through its open, year-round grantmaking approach, the foundation supports innovative, community-driven solutions that address systemic challenges such as education, health, food security, economic opportunity, and racial equity . As outlined in the official grant guidance , the foundation is committed to supporting organizations that are building a future where every child and family has access to opportunity, resources, and wellbeing. About the W.K. Kellogg Foundation Grant Program The W.K. Kellogg Foundation is a globally recognized philanthropic organization focused on improving outcomes for vulnerable children, families, and communities . The foundation envisions a world where: Children receive nurturing early care and education Families have access to quality healthcare Communities enjoy equitable food systems Parents and caregivers have sustainable career pathways Societies heal from systemic inequities and racial disparities Through its grants, WKKF partners with organizations that are actively working to make this vision a reality. Key Focus Areas of Funding WKKF prioritizes funding initiatives that align with its core mission areas: 1. Early Childhood Development and Education Access to quality early learning environments Support systems for children and families Programs that improve developmental outcomes 2. Maternal and Child Health Accessible healthcare for mothers and babies Community-based health interventions Programs improving maternal and infant wellbeing 3. Food Security and Agricultural Support Access to nutritious and affordable food Support for farmers and food systems Sustainable agricultural initiatives 4. Economic Opportunity and Workforce Development Career pathways for parents and caregivers Job creation and entrepreneurship support Financial stability programs 5. Racial Equity and Community Healing Initiatives addressing systemic racism Community-led solutions for equity Programs promoting social cohesion and justice Where the Foundation Funds The W.K. Kellogg Foundation focuses its grantmaking efforts in specific geographic regions: United States: Nationwide support Strategic focus in: Michigan Mississippi New Mexico New Orleans International: Mexico (Chiapas and the Yucatán Peninsula) Haiti (central and southwest regions) Organizations working in these regions or addressing similar issues are strongly encouraged to apply. Types of Projects Supported WKKF supports a wide range of community-driven initiatives, including: Nonprofit programs and interventions Community-based development projects Policy and advocacy initiatives Research and innovation projects Capacity-building programs What the Foundation Does NOT Fund It is important to note that WKKF does not provide funding for: Individuals Capital investments (e.g., infrastructure projects) Political parties or candidates Applicants should ensure their proposals align with these guidelines. Grant Benefits and Value While funding amounts vary depending on the project, selected organizations benefit from: Financial support for impactful programs Strategic partnership with a global foundation Access to knowledge and resources Opportunities for long-term collaboration Application Process The W.K. Kellogg Foundation uses a flexible, rolling application process , meaning there are no strict deadlines . Step-by-Step Application Guide: Understand Funding Priorities Review WKKF’s focus areas and geographic priorities Register in the Grants System (Fluxx) Create an account in the foundation’s grant management platform Submit a Letter of Inquiry (LOI) Provide a high-level overview of your project Clearly describe your impact and objectives Initial Review Applicants receive feedback within 30 business days Full Proposal Submission (If Invited) Develop a detailed proposal Collaborate with WKKF staff if needed Final Review and Decision Funding decisions are typically made within 60 business days Get step-by-step instructions for registering in Fluxx and preparing and submitting your LOI, including prompts to focus your request. Instructions are available in English , Spanish , and Haitian Creole . Click HERE to submit an application. Commitment to Grantseekers The W.K. Kellogg Foundation is known for its transparent and supportive application process. The foundation commits to: Clear communication and responsiveness Fair and respectful evaluation of proposals Providing feedback on declined applications Continuous improvement through applicant feedback Why You Should Apply The WKKF grant program stands out because it: Has no fixed deadlines (rolling applications) Supports long-term, systemic change Focuses on equity and community empowerment Encourages innovative and locally driven solutions Provides access to a global philanthropic network Real Impact Example One of the foundation’s grantees, the National Young Farmers Coalition , has successfully: Supported over 200,000 farmers and ranchers Improved access to farmland Strengthened food systems and entrepreneurship This highlights the scale of impact possible through WKKF funding. Important Notes Applications are open year-round No official deadline Early submission is encouraged for timely review Click HERE to learn more about the W.K. Kellogg Foundation Grants 2026. Conclusion The W.K. Kellogg Foundation Grant 2026 is a powerful opportunity for organizations committed to advancing equity, improving child and family wellbeing, and building resilient communities. With its flexible application process, strong support system, and focus on impactful change, this grant program is ideal for organizations seeking to scale their initiatives and drive sustainable transformation. Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY HERE . The post W.K. Kellogg Foundation Grants 2026: Open Funding Opportunities for Organizations Advancing Equity, Child Wellbeing, and Community Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ICFJ+ Fellowship 2026: Reporting on Literacy and Numeracy in Africa – Fully Funded Opportunity for Journalists in Kenya, Senegal, and South Africa
summary: The crisis in foundational learning across sub-Saharan Africa remains one of the most urgent yet underreported challenges facing the continent today. With an estimated 9 out of 10 children unable to read for meaning or perform basic mathematical operations by the age of 10, the implications for economic development, social mobility, and long-term national progress are profound. While evidence-based solutions to improve learning outcomes exist and political will is gradually strengthening, the pace of implementation across countries remains insufficient. Recognizing the powerful role of journalism in shaping public discourse and influencing policy, ICFJ+ has launched an impactful initiative aimed at strengthening education reporting across Africa. The Reporting on Literacy and Numeracy Fellowship 2026 is designed to equip journalists, content creators, and media professionals with the tools, knowledge, and support needed to produce compelling, data-driven, and solutions-oriented stories that can drive change. About the Fellowship Program This prestigious fellowship targets media professionals based in Kenya, Senegal, and South Africa, offering a unique opportunity to deepen their understanding of foundational learning challenges while enhancing their storytelling capacity. Over a six-month period, selected fellows will participate in a comprehensive program that combines training, mentorship, and hands-on reporting experience. The initiative focuses on improving coverage of literacy and numeracy issues, ensuring that reporting not only highlights problems but also explores practical solutions and amplifies voices from affected communities. What the Fellowship Offers Participants selected for the program will benefit from a range of valuable resources and opportunities, including: Reporting Grants: Financial support to produce in-depth, high-quality stories on literacy and numeracy. Individual Mentorship: Guidance from experienced journalists and subject-matter experts throughout the fellowship. Story Production Support: Editorial assistance to refine and publish impactful stories. Training Sessions: Capacity-building workshops focused on education data, pedagogy, and solutions journalism. Study Tours: Opportunities to gain exposure to successful education interventions and reporting practices. Professional Networking: Access to a network of like-minded journalists, educators, and policy influencers. The program is designed not just to enhance storytelling but to ensure that participants produce work that reaches key stakeholders, including policymakers, educators, and communities directly affected by learning gaps. Who Should Apply The fellowship is open to a diverse range of media professionals, including: Journalists working with established media organizations Freelance reporters Content creators and digital storytellers Social media influencers with a focus on public interest issues Applicants must be based in Kenya, Senegal, or South Africa and should demonstrate a strong interest in education, social policy, or development reporting. While prior experience in education reporting is an advantage, it is not a strict requirement. What Makes a Strong Application To stand out, applicants should clearly articulate: Their understanding of gaps in current coverage of foundational learning in their country A compelling story idea or reporting angle Their strategy for reaching and engaging policymakers, educators, and affected communities A commitment to producing impactful journalism beyond traditional publication formats The fellowship prioritizes individuals who are not only skilled storytellers but also passionate about driving meaningful change through their work. Application Timeline Applications Open: April 10, 2026 Application Deadline: May 10, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure that all required materials are submitted before the deadline. Why This Fellowship Matters At its core, this initiative seeks to transform how foundational learning issues are reported in Africa. By empowering journalists with data literacy, storytelling tools, and access to expert insights, the program aims to elevate education reporting from surface-level narratives to impactful journalism that informs, influences, and inspires action. In a region where millions of children are at risk of being left behind, the role of the media is critical. This fellowship represents an opportunity for journalists to become catalysts for change, using their platforms to spotlight solutions, hold systems accountable, and ultimately contribute to improving learning outcomes across the continent. Application Deadline: May 10, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here The post ICFJ+ Fellowship 2026: Reporting on Literacy and Numeracy in Africa – Fully Funded Opportunity for Journalists in Kenya, Senegal, and South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Research Experience Placements (REPs) Summer 2026 at the University of Edinburgh: Paid Research Opportunity for Students
summary: If you re an undergraduate or integrated Master s student looking to gain hands-on research experience this summer, the Research Experience Placements (REPs) for Summer 2026 at the University of Edinburgh present an outstanding opportunity. Funded by the Natural Environment Research Council (NERC) under the E5 Doctoral Training Partnership, these placements are designed to help students explore careers in environmental sciences through real-world research projects. Why You Should Apply The REPs program offers more than just a summer activity—it provides a meaningful step toward a future in research or postgraduate study. Participants will: Work on real research projects supervised by leading academics Receive mentorship from current PhD students Gain practical skills in environmental science research Build confidence and clarify career pathways Earn a salary above the National Living Wage This is a highly valuable experience, particularly for students considering postgraduate study or research-focused careers. Placement Overview Duration: 7–8 weeks (June–August 2026) Working Options: Full-time or part-time Salary: Paid at University of Edinburgh Grade 3.1 (pro-rata from £24,729/year) Location: Edinburgh and partner institutions including the UK Centre for Ecology Hydrology (UKCEH) and the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh Start Date: Earliest start from 15 June 2026 Please note that participants must be physically based in the UK for the duration of the placement. Research Projects Available Students can apply to one project only. Topics include: Wildlife ecology and animal behavior Soil chemistry and hydrogen measurement Climate and glacier research Plant taxonomy and biodiversity Agricultural sustainability Marine ecology and oxygen dynamics Conservation strategies for tropical forests Each project is supervised by an expert in the field, offering a unique opportunity to contribute to impactful research. Eligibility Criteria To be eligible, applicants must: Be enrolled in their first undergraduate degree (including integrated Master s) at a UK Higher Education Institution Not have previously completed a NERC REP Meet NERC PhD funding eligibility requirements Have the right to work or study in the UK for the full placement duration Students in their final year are eligible if they have not graduated before the placement begins. Commitment to Inclusion The program strongly encourages applications from students from underrepresented backgrounds, including: Ethnic minority groups Students with disabilities Individuals from low-income backgrounds Applicants may submit a Widening Participation statement to provide context about their experiences. Important Considerations No funding is provided for relocation or accommodation Applicants must ensure they can commute to the placement location A final report must be submitted at the end of the program Application Process To apply, submit the following documents via email: Completed application form Academic reference Interim academic transcript Equality and Diversity Monitoring form Optional: Widening Participation statement Deadline: 12:00 Noon (UK time), Friday 1 May 2026 Applications must be complete at the time of submission. How to Apply Find full details and application materials here: APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more internship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts The Research Experience Placements at the University of Edinburgh offer a rare chance to immerse yourself in meaningful research while earning a salary. With limited placements available, early preparation is essential. If you re serious about a future in environmental science or research, this opportunity could be a turning point. title: Master of Public Administration (MPA) at the African School of Governance (ASG): A Transformative Leadership Programme for Africa’s Future
summary: Shape Governance. Drive Impact. Lead Africa Forward. If you are passionate about transforming public systems, influencing policy, and leading with purpose, the Master of Public Administration (MPA) at the African School of Governance (ASG) offers a powerful pathway to impact. Designed specifically for Africa’s realities, this programme prepares a new generation of leaders equipped to tackle the continent’s most pressing governance and development challenges. With a strong emphasis on leadership, innovation, and practical application, this two-year graduate programme goes beyond traditional academic learning to cultivate ethical, strategic, and action-oriented public leaders. 🎓 Programme Overview The ASG MPA is a two-year, 120-credit programme structured to build both foundational knowledge and advanced leadership capacity. Year 1: Foundations of Leadership, Policy, and Institutions Students develop a solid grounding in: Public policy analysis Institutional economics Political economy Research methods Leadership for public service Outcome: A deep understanding of how governance systems, leadership, and economic structures influence development outcomes across Africa. Year 2: Specialization, Strategy, and Impact Students move into advanced learning and application through: Specialized tracks Policy-focused coursework Real-world engagement 🌱 Experiential Learning Opportunities Work Integrated Learning (WIL) Students gain hands-on experience through placements in: Government ministries NGOs Regional organizations Private-sector institutions Capstone Project A supervised project where students: Develop policy solutions Propose institutional reforms Conduct applied research 💡 Key Skills and Competencies Developed Graduates of the ASG MPA programme will: Lead with integrity and a pan-African mindset Design and implement evidence-based policies Navigate complex governance and political systems Manage public institutions effectively Drive inclusive and accountable governance 🌟 Why Choose the ASG MPA? Africa-Centered Curriculum Rooted in African contexts, histories, and development priorities. Leadership Development Focus Emphasizes ethical leadership, purpose-driven action, and public service values. Hands-On Learning Approach Combines case studies, simulations, and fieldwork for real-world readiness. Global Faculty Partnerships Learn from leading African and international experts. Pan-African Network Join a vibrant community of emerging leaders across the continent. 💰 Scholarships Available Both merit-based and need-based scholarships are offered to support qualified candidates. 📝 Application Process Steps to Apply Select your programme from the official portal Start your application Complete all required information Submit your application Receive your Application ID via email Proceed with payment Track your application status 📄 Required Documents National ID (for Rwandans aged 18+) Valid passport (for non-Rwandans) Bachelor’s degree certificate Academic transcripts Updated CV/Resume Proof of English proficiency (if applicable) 💳 Application Fee $50 USD (non-refundable) 📅 Application Deadline 15 May 2026 APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here . 📧 Support For assistance, contact: support@asg.ac The post Master of Public Administration (MPA) at the African School of Governance (ASG): A Transformative Leadership Programme for Africa’s Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Remote Research Officer Job at Southern Voice (2026) – Earn Up to $2,200/Month
summary: If you’re passionate about global development, policy research, and amplifying voices from the Global South, this opportunity could be exactly what you’ve been waiting for. Southern Voice is currently hiring a Research Officer to join its dynamic international team in a fully remote capacity. This role offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of research, policy, and global collaboration—supporting impactful, multi-country initiatives that influence global development conversations. About Southern Voice Southern Voice is a network of 71 think tanks across the Global South, committed to generating high-quality, evidence-based research to inform global policy dialogues, especially around the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The organization plays a critical role in ensuring that perspectives from the Global South shape international decision-making processes. Position Overview The Research Officer will work closely with the Head of Research Innovation to coordinate and implement research initiatives, manage publications, and contribute to knowledge production. This is a remote, full-time consultancy role with a strong focus on research coordination, stakeholder engagement, and policy-relevant outputs. Location: Remote (Home-based) Start Date: June 8, 2026 (earliest) Contract Duration: Initial 3-month probation, followed by a 1-year consultancy (renewable) Salary: USD 2,000 – 2,200 per month Key Responsibilities The selected candidate will: Coordinate multi-country research projects and initiatives Produce high-quality research outputs (reports, briefs, concept notes) Manage publication processes including peer review and dissemination Organize meetings, workshops, and collaborative activities Engage with global stakeholders and research partners Support institutional planning and strategic research development Contribute to partnerships and global policy dialogues Why You Should Apply Working with Southern Voice means being part of a globally recognized network influencing development policy. This role provides: Exposure to international policy and research ecosystems Opportunities to collaborate with global experts and institutions A platform to amplify Global South perspectives Hands-on experience in impactful, policy-driven research Requirements To be considered, applicants should have: A Bachelor’s degree in International Development, Political Science, Economics, or related fields At least 3 years of experience in research or policy-related roles Strong analytical, writing, and coordination skills Experience managing research projects or initiatives Excellent English communication skills Ability to work across time zones (minimum 5-hour overlap with UTC+5:30) Application Process Applicants must submit: A 2-page CV A 1-page cover letter (in English) addressing: Why you are a strong fit Your understanding of Southern Voice’s work How your experience aligns with the role Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is highly encouraged. Deadline May 8, 2026 (12:00 PM CET) Note: The position may be filled before the deadline due to rolling review. How to Apply APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . title: Peering Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Opportunity for Internet Infrastructure Professionals
summary: The 2026 Peering Fellowship is now open for applications, offering a unique opportunity for Internet infrastructure professionals to strengthen their expertise, expand global networks, and contribute to building a more resilient and accessible Internet ecosystem. Launched by the Internet Society, this six-month fellowship program is designed to empower professionals working in peering and interconnection with the tools, knowledge, and global exposure needed to tackle real-world Internet challenges. Application Deadline Applications close on 17 May 2026 at 23:59 UTC . Early application is strongly encouraged. About the Peering Fellowship In an increasingly interconnected world, the efficiency and resilience of the Internet depend heavily on strong peering and interconnection systems. The Peering Fellowship supports professionals working to improve Internet access by enhancing local interconnection infrastructure and advocating for supportive policy environments. This program selects 20 fellows annually and provides a structured blend of virtual learning and in-person engagement at major regional peering events. Program Structure and What to Expect Participants in the Peering Fellowship will engage in a comprehensive learning experience that includes: Virtual Learning Sessions: Interactive online sessions, workshops, and collaborative discussions In-Person Peering Event Attendance: Fellows will attend a regional peering event aligned with their location Technical Training: Deep dives into Internet routing, Internet Exchange Points (IXPs), and interconnection strategies Policy and Advocacy Training: Understanding the regulatory landscape shaping Internet access Networking Opportunities: Direct engagement with global industry leaders and peers Local Impact Projects: Fellows will apply their learning to improve Internet infrastructure in their communities Fellowship Benefits The Peering Fellowship offers both professional and financial support, including: Round-trip airfare to a regional peering event Hotel accommodation during the event Stipend for additional expenses Access to a global network of Internet professionals Advanced training from experienced industry experts Opportunity to contribute to improving Internet access and resilience Key Program Timeline (2026) 27 April: Applications open 17 May: Applications close 18 May – 19 June: Application review period Late June: Selection notifications July – December: Fellowship program duration August – November: Regional peering events December: Program concludes Regional Peering Events Fellows will attend one of the following events based on their region: Africa: AfPIF (18–21 August, Kigali, Rwanda) Latin America Caribbean: LACNIC/LACNOG (19–22 October, Mendoza, Argentina) Asia-Pacific: Peering Asia (2–6 November, Osaka, Japan) Middle East: Event to be announced Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be based in Africa, Asia-Pacific, Latin America and the Caribbean, or the Middle East Have at least 3 years of experience in Internet infrastructure or related fields Possess knowledge or involvement in routing, IXPs, peering, or Internet policy Be able to commit 4–6 hours weekly throughout the program Have reliable Internet access and necessary devices Be available for sessions between 12:00 and 16:00 UTC Demonstrate basic English communication skills Not have participated in recent Internet Society fellowships (2024–present) Selection Process Applications will undergo a two-stage review process conducted by independent committees. The process is anonymized, ensuring fairness and impartiality. All applicants will receive a notification regarding their selection status after the review period. Why You Should Apply The Peering Fellowship is more than just a training program—it’s a gateway to becoming part of a global movement dedicated to improving Internet accessibility, affordability, and resilience. Fellows gain practical experience, expand their professional networks, and contribute to meaningful change in their regions. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare their submissions before the deadline and ensure they meet all eligibility requirements. For inquiries, applicants can reach out via email: fellowships@isoc.org . APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here .
